summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Benzene (C₆H₆) is an example of:

Benzene: C₆H₆ with delocalized π electrons in a ring. Aromatic compounds are cyclic, conjugated, and unusually stable due to aromaticity.

Example: The ester linkage (-COO-) is found in:

Polyesters contain -COO- ester linkages throughout the chain. PET (polyethylene terephthalate) = Dacron/Mylar. Triglycerides (fats) are esters of glycerol with fatty acids.
